title: Avoidable Blindness And Cataract Surgical Coverage Gaps In Low- And Middle-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring avoidable blindness prevalence and cataract surgical coverage gaps in LMICs, linking RAAB survey data, eye-health workforce data, and facility-location data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: WHO estimates 2.2 billion people have vision impairment, of whom 1 billion cases are preventable or unaddressed. Cataract accounts for 50 percent of blindness in LMICs. Cataract surgical coverage is below 50 percent in most low-income countries. RAAB surveys provide gold-standard sub-national blindness data, but linking survey data to eye-health workforce and facility data is underdeveloped.

success_metrics.target: Every CSC estimate links survey, workforce, and facility data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
